candidates.name: Bob Ferguson
candidates.notes: Sworn in as governor January 2025. Former WA Attorney General (2013-2025). As AG, filed first lawsuit against Trump travel ban. Strong gun control advocate — pushed assault weapons ban in WA. Supports abortion access. Opposes school choice. Filed lawsuits against crisis pregnancy centers.

profile.education: University of Washington (BA, Political Science); New York University (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; King County Council member; Washington Attorney General (2013-2025); became 24th governor in January 2025.

candidates.name: Patty Murray
candidates.notes: Senator since 1993. Senate President pro tempore (2023-2025). Heritage Action 0% current, 4% lifetime. NRA F rated. Pro-choice champion — led Senate efforts to protect abortion access. Supports assault weapons ban. Supports DREAM Act. Opposes border wall. Known as 'mom in tennis shoes' from her grassroots start.

profile.background: Former preschool teacher and Washington State Senator. Self-described 'mom in tennis shoes.' Senate President pro tempore. In Senate since 1993. Former Appropriations Committee chair.
profile.office: 154 Russell Senate Office Building

aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

candidates.name: Maria Cantwell
candidates.notes: Senator since 2001. Heritage Action 0% current, 5% lifetime. NRA F rated. Former tech executive (RealNetworks VP). Pro-choice. Supports assault weapons ban and red flag laws. Supports DREAM Act and comprehensive immigration reform. Chairs Senate Commerce Committee. Focus on tech policy, trade, and energy.

profile.education: Miami University of Ohio (BA Public Administration)
profile.background: Former tech executive at RealNetworks. Former Washington State legislator and U.S. House member. Worked in high-tech industry before returning to politics for Senate run in 2000.
profile.office: 511 Hart Senate Office Building
